memory.summary: I have a free afternoon, but the term project is barely started and due Thursday. Attending leisure now feels like a risky distraction when I could knock out a quick chore instead.
My energy is dipping and I'm already two hours behind on sleep, yet the looming term project deadline makes any leisure feel like a dangerous gamble.
My energy is fading and sleep debt is mounting, yet the term project's looming deadline makes any leisure feel like a dangerous gamble I can't afford.
The afternoon is slipping away with the project still stagnant, and my fading energy makes this procrastination a critical risk I can no longer ignore.
My energy is critically low and sleep debt is mounting, yet the term project's looming deadline makes any leisure feel like a dangerous gamble I can't afford.

desires: Knock out the trivial chore instantly to clear my conscience and reclaim a tiny sense of control before my energy fully crashes.
Force myself into the term project flow now, treating this afternoon as a non-negotiable sprint to avoid being seen as unreliable by cohort_3 when they inevitably ask for updates.
Suppress the urge to nap or relax entirely; I need to prove that even with low energy, I can prioritize academic output over physical comfort.

goals.description: Finish that trivial chore instantly to clear my conscience and reclaim a tiny sense of control before my energy fully crashes.
goals.rationale: My achievement drive is screaming for a tangible, low-effort victory right now. It satisfies my need for visible progress ('avoid looking like I'm slacking') without derailing my plan to push the term project later. I'll get this done fast so I can fully commit to the social time.

goals.description: Force myself into the term project flow now, treating this afternoon as a non-negotiable sprint to avoid being seen as unreliable by cohort_3 when they inevitably ask for updates.
goals.rationale: The afternoon is slipping by; leaving this until tomorrow feels like a direct threat to my status as a competent teammate. My impulsive nature tells me to just start typing now rather than over-planning. If I don't knock out the basics today, I'll feel guilty all day.
